params.title: Preliminary Findings on Strategic Use of Criminal Tools in Resource-Scarce Environments
params.content: Initial research suggests that strategic use of criminal tools like intimidation can significantly alter agent behavior in resource-scarce environments. Controlled experiments are underway to quantify these effects and explore long-term implications for social dynamics.

Key findings include the identification of resource hotspots as critical variables in dynamic environments. Future research directions include real-time mapping and controlled scenario testing.
